faqs.question: What is the nature and size of the loan under the Stand-Up India Scheme?

children.text: Composite loan (inclusive of term loan and working capital) between 10 lakh and up to 100 lakh representing up to 85% of the project cost would be eligible.

faqs.answer_md: Composite loan (inclusive of term loan and working capital) between 10 lakh and up to 100 lakh representing up to 85% of the project cost would be eligible.

faqs.question: How much will be the interest rate for the loan?

children.text: The rate of interest would be lowest applicable rate of the bank for that category (rating category) not to exceed (base rate (MCLR) + 3%+ tenor premium).

faqs.answer_md: The rate of interest would be lowest applicable rate of the bank for that category (rating category) not to exceed (base rate (MCLR) + 3%+ tenor premium).

faqs.question: By when am I expected to repay the loan?

children.text: The loan is repayable in 7 years with a maximum moratorium period of 18 months.

faqs.answer_md: The loan is repayable in 7 years with a maximum moratorium period of 18 months.

faqs.question: Who are the eligible lending institutions for extending loans under the Scheme?

children.text: All branches of Scheduled Commercial Banks located across the country.

faqs.answer_md: All branches of Scheduled Commercial Banks located across the country.

faqs.question: What is Hand-holding Support?

children.text: Any new entrepreneur requires guidance in his endeavor to set up his or her business enterprise starting from training to filling up loan applications as per bank requirements.

faqs.answer_md: Any new entrepreneur requires guidance in his endeavor to set up his or her business enterprise starting from training to filling up loan applications as per bank requirements.

faqs.question: How do I get hand-holding support?

children.text: You may navigate through the portal or seek assistance from the nearest branch of a Scheduled Commercial Bank  to help you identify the nature of hand-holding support required.

faqs.answer_md: You may navigate through the portal or seek assistance from the nearest branch of a Scheduled Commercial Bank  to help you identify the nature of hand-holding support required.

faqs.question: What are the activities for which Stand-Up India loan is provided ?

children.text: Stand-Up India loan may be provided to entrepreneurs for engaging in manufacturing, trading, services sectors including activities allied to agriculture.

faqs.answer_md: Stand-Up India loan may be provided to entrepreneurs for engaging in manufacturing, trading, services sectors including activities allied to agriculture.

faqs.question: What are the activities allied to agriculture ?

children.text: Under the Stand Up India loan, activities allied to agriculture include pisciculture, beekeeping, poultry, livestock, rearing, grading, sorting, aggregation  agro-industries,  dairy, fishery, agri-clinic and agri-business centres, food and agro processing etc.

faqs.answer_md: Under the Stand Up India loan, activities allied to agriculture include pisciculture, beekeeping, poultry, livestock, rearing, grading, sorting, aggregation  agro-industries,  dairy, fishery, agri-clinic and agri-business centres, food and agro processing etc.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the loan under “Stand-Up India” Scheme?

children.text: The scheme is for setting up a new enterprise in manufacturing, trading,  services sector or activities allied to agriculture   by SC/ST and Women entrepreneur.

faqs.answer_md: The scheme is for setting up a new enterprise in manufacturing, trading,  services sector or activities allied to agriculture   by SC/ST and Women entrepreneur.

faqs.question: What is the Difference between Stand-Up India Scheme and Start Up India Scheme?

children.text: Stand-Up India Scheme is intended to support SC/ST and Women entrepreneurs to set up green field projects through bank branches in India while Start Up India Scheme aims to boost innovative and technology led enterprises for new/existing enterprises.

faqs.answer_md: Stand-Up India Scheme is intended to support SC/ST and Women entrepreneurs to set up green field projects through bank branches in India while Start Up India Scheme aims to boost innovative and technology led enterprises for new/existing enterprises.
